reason: A model-local Python 3.10 venv was the lowest-risk executable backend on this host. It matched the upstream development install path and avoided unavailable uv/docker backends.
evidence: Runtime preflight found no uv binary and no docker daemon.
The upstream README exposes a development path based on python=3.9 plus pip install -r requirements.txt.
Phase-1 only requires import/load/infer on CPU and does not justify a heavier backend.

rejected_options.reason: Unavailable on host and unnecessary for the requested CPU-only minimal callable path.
Not supported by repo-native evidence and unnecessary for this phase-1 target.
Model is local and the target path is repo-native.

evidence_conflicts.reason: Executable host evidence selected Python 3.10 because it was available in a reusable local interpreter without mutating the host environment.
